performance_overview: Through 7 sessions (inception 2026-06-01) still only 2 'completed trades' exist and neither is a clean signal-driven round-trip: trade 1 = the 2026-06-04 MPC long (-0.61%, stopped ~1 min after entry on a too-tight, ultra-low-ATR stop), trade 2 = the 2026-06-05 corrective BUY-TO-COVER of the unintended -18 MPC short created by the 06-04 EOD double-sell (+0.54%). So the 1W/1L (50%) framing is state-repair noise, not edge; effective clean-signal sample is still ~0. Today (06-09) Bull was flat (+$0.00, 0.00%) while SPY closed -0.79%, so +0.79% relative on the day purely from holding cash. Since inception cumulative Bull +0.0067% vs SPY -3.31%: Bull is well ahead of SPY cumulatively, but entirely because it has stayed flat/cautious through a risk-off stretch, not from realized trading gains. Trend over the last 5 sessions: five straight no-trade/flat days, selectivity beating SPY cumulatively while clean-signal sample remains zero.
overnight_hold_insights: Still ZERO genuine overnight holds completed across all 7 sessions. The overnight_holds.closed_eod bucket reads 2 trades / 1W / 50% / avg -0.035%, but it is contaminated: both entries are intraday/EOD closes (the 06-04 MPC long close and the 06-05 corrective short-cover), neither a real carry-overnight outcome. No gap-risk data exists yet. Carry-decision policy unchanged: apply the 6 config safe-hold gates (profitable, trailing stop active, price > EMA-21, no earnings within 2 days, sentiment not negative, gap-risk cushion). Standing lesson from MPC: very low-ATR, tight-stop names that don't extend within the session are correctly closed rather than carried.
recent_market_context: 06-09 (Tue): a two-sided, choppy risk-off session. Premarket the YTD-momentum/semicap names rebounded hard (AMD +5.0%, TSLA +4.6%, AVGO +2.8%, NVDA +1.7%, energy cluster OXY/CVX/XOM/MPC green), VIX 18.14 easing, SPY regime trending_up at the open. But the rebound FAILED to confirm intraday and unwound: by 12:32 ET SPY was -1.47% with semicap round-tripping (AMD 490->446), then SPY recovered into the close to finish -0.79%. EMA9<EMA21 with sub-50 RSI dominated the momentum complex most of the day. 25-name watchlist, all positive sentiment, 0 screened out, earnings blacklist EMPTY. Both open (10:09) and midday (12:32) agents found ZERO clean passes; the morning rel_vol near-misses (TNK/JPM/PANW) all rolled over. Book FLAT all day (5th straight flat session).
